findings.description: Phantom env:KEY secret-slot marker on attached-form --flag=value credentials: SECRET_ENV_ASSIGNMENT's leading \b treats the '-'->letter boundary inside '--api-key=' as a word boundary, matching the interior 'key=...' segment (ENV .sub runs before FLAG .sub), appending a bogus 'env:' slot. Reproduced: --api-key=secretval -> secret_slots == ['env:KEY','flag:api-key']; --auth-token=abc -> ['env:TOKEN','flag:auth-token']. No secret leaks and secret_affected True regardless; data-quality defect in the serialized typed markers. Fix: replace leading \b with negative lookbehind (?<![-\w]) at line ~275. Reordering FLAG before ENV does NOT fix it.

New Python gh-caller absent from the #245 static routing pin loop: lib/test/run.sh's DGH_PY loop lists the 'four Python gh-callers' (workpad.py file-deferrals.py match-deferrals.py parse-acs.py) but export-workflow-lifecycle-census.py is a genuine fifth (--gh default os.environ.get('DEVFLOW_GH') or 'gh' at line 353); a revert to bare 'gh' would be caught by no pin. Coupled-site coverage gap per CLAUDE.md's gh-resolver gotcha. Fix: add it to the loop (and reconcile the 'four' count comment).

cleared: all doc claims cross-checked accurate; no documented_falsehood; SPDX present
sub_threshold: cloud rows store {conclusion,status} under host_profile blended with local provider/model/branch — naming/altitude smell, no downstream count affected (~70)
